title: 2026 Oscars Best Supporting Actor Rankings
text: Jacob Elordi, Frankenstein ranks fifth. The critic acknowledges Elordi's excellent (and film-saving) work and praises his physical performance as the Creature. However, among these five nominees, this ranking represents the least compelling case for the Oscar. Delroy Lindo, Sinners ranks fourth. Lindo arrives as this year's Best Supporting Actor wildcard without major precursor recognition. The review highlights his emotional depth, particularly noting how he transforms anguish into music in real time, making his performance the soul of the first half. Stellan Skarsgård, Sentimental Value ranks third. Skarsgård delivers simultaneously familiar from his previous work and excitingly rich acting. The performance masterfully conveys emotional depth while appearing withdrawn—showing a full spectrum of unguarded emotion despite the character's closed-off nature. Benicio del Toro, One Battle After Another ranks second. Del Toro provides reflective centeredness amid chaos, serving as the immovable object. His work is described as delicate, unshowy yet extremely memorable, balancing moral conviction with subtle comedic timing. Sean Penn, One Battle After Another ranks first. Penn's grotesque, cartoonish performance as Lockjaw ultimately prevails. The critic argues Penn captures the satire's core: recognizable fascism beneath theatrical excess, with the character's pathology oozing through the screen.
